meaning: adj.<古><苏格兰>忠实的,诚实的
examples: Don't let them leal you by the nose.
Lizards in the wild capture moving prey by running up and down trees, Dr. Leal said.
House Martell is ancient and noble, and has been a leal friend to House Targaryen for more than a century, Your Grace.
Lulis Leal, 47, of New York, is also getting in on the pi memorization action.
"It isn't easy, brother, " Leal said as Chavez supporters headed to the river to watch the cortege pass.
